reasoning: The vfx_dust_poof_v02 particle renders as a flat, dark cloud shape to the left of the player character. Player reference appears ~60-70px tall in screen space; the dust poof spans roughly 90-100px wide x 35px tall — wider than the player horizontally. For a ground-level particle effect this footprint is borderline: the flat oval shape and low profile correctly suggest a top-down ground-dust cue and do not overwhelm the scene, but the horizontal spread slightly exceeds what a tightly-scoped 'particle' category should occupy relative to a 32x32 player. Perspective matches the top-down 2D style and the toy-diorama feel is preserved (no wallpaper-scale inflation). Main concern is lateral width — trimming the poof to ~70% of current width would bring it cleanly within category bounds.
